original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Urological Association and Society of Urodynamics, Female Pelvic Medicine & Urogenital Reconstruction
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with microhaematuria, with risk, infection, kidney-disease, anticoagulant, gynaecological, and follow-up qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial evaluation, risk-based urological evaluation, and follow-up of microhaematuria.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Partial for a broad haematuria workflow. Exact for selected microhaematuria definition, gross-haematuria history, urinary symptoms, risk factors, urinalysis, blood pressure, creatinine, and qualified follow-up documentation. It does not support automatic cancer conclusions, imaging, cystoscopy, referral, or UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Diagnosis and definition of microhaematuria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis with microscopic confirmation and microhaematuria terminology without assigning cause.

exact_sections.heading: Initial evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports history, physical examination, blood pressure, creatinine, gross-haematuria history, irritative urinary symptoms, smoking and occupational exposure, family history, anticoagulant context, urinary infection context, and flank-pain context.

exact_sections.heading: Follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician shared-decision follow-up and reassessment when gross haematuria or new urinary symptoms occur, without automatic investigation or referral output.

title: Microhematuria: AUA/SUFU Guideline (2020, Amended 2025)
publisher: American Urological Association and Society of Urodynamics, Female Pelvic Medicine & Urogenital Reconstruction
population: Adults with microhaematuria, with risk, infection, kidney-disease, anticoagulant, gynaecological, and follow-up qualifiers.
intended_setting: Initial evaluation, risk-based urological evaluation, and follow-up of microhaematuria.
